input.command: cd "${HOME}/Library/Application Support/Open Design/namespaces/release-stable/data/projects/proj-001" && CHROME="/Applications/Google Chrome.app/Contents/MacOS/Google Chrome" && mkdir -p exports && for spec in "feed-1x1-a:1080:1080" "feed-1x1-b:1080:1080" "feed-4x5-a:1080:1350" "feed-4x5-b:1080:1350" "stories-9x16-a:1080:1920" "stories-9x16-b:1080:1920" "carousel-a:4380:1080" "carousel-b:4380:1080"; do name="${spec%%:*}"; rest="${spec#*:}"; w="${rest%:*}"; h="${rest#*:}"; echo "rendering $name ${w}x${h}"; "$CHROME" --headless --disable-gpu --no-sandbox --hide-scrollbars --virtual-time-budget=15000 --window-size=$w,$h --screenshot="exports/$name.png" "file://$PWD/$name.html" 2>/dev/null; done && ls -la exports/

output: File created successfully at: ${HOME}/Library/Application Support/Open Design/namespaces/release-stable/data/projects/proj-001/print-a4.html (file state is current in your context — no need to Read it back)

input.command: cd "${HOME}/Library/Application Support/Open Design/namespaces/release-stable/data/projects/proj-001" && CHROME="/Applications/Google Chrome.app/Contents/MacOS/Google Chrome" && "$CHROME" --headless --disable-gpu --no-sandbox --no-pdf-header-footer --virtual-time-budget=15000 --print-to-pdf=index.pdf --print-to-pdf-no-header "file://$PWD/print-a4.html" 2>&1 | tail -3 && ls -la index.pdf && gs -sDEVICE=pdfwrite -dCompatibilityLevel=1.5 -dPDFSETTINGS=/printer -dNOPAUSE -dBATCH -sOutputFile=index-compressed.pdf index.pdf >/dev/null 2>&1 && mv index-compressed.pdf index.pdf && ls -la index.pdf

content: The issue is iframes — Chrome's print engine doesn't reliably wait for nested iframe images. I'll render each ad to a PNG first, then build a print-ready A4 page that embeds those PNGs directly (no iframes).Done — `index.pdf` is now A4 portrait, 1.3MB, with all 8 ad creatives embedded as actual images (not iframes). `★ Insight ─────────────────────────────────────` - Root cause of the empty PDF: Chrome's print engine treats `
